 {"id":"bd-t51x","title":"Audit ntm for package distribution","description":"# Audit: ntm (Named Tmux Manager) for Package Distribution

## Tool Overview

**Repository**: /data/projects/ntm (https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ntm)
**Language**: Go
**Purpose**: Orchestrate AI coding agents in tmux sessions with named, persistent configurations

ntm is a Go-based CLI tool that helps manage multiple AI coding agent sessions in tmux. It provides:
- Named session management
- Persistent configurations
- Multi-agent orchestration
- Integration with various AI coding tools (Claude Code, Codex, etc.)

## Audit Checklist

### 1. Binary Distribution Readiness
- [ ] Check if Go project with main package
- [ ] Check for existing GitHub releases
- [ ] Check if releases include pre-built binaries
- [ ] Identify target platforms (should support all: macOS Intel/ARM, Linux x64/ARM, Windows)

### 2. GoReleaser Status
- [ ] Check for existing .goreleaser.yml or .goreleaser.yaml
- [ ] If missing, flag for GoReleaser setup task
- [ ] Check for GitHub Actions release workflow

### 3. CLI Interface
- [ ] Verify has CLI entry point
- [ ] Check for --version flag support
- [ ] Check for --help flag support
- [ ] Note any runtime dependencies (tmux required)

### 4. Documentation
- [ ] Check README for installation section
- [ ] Note any special installation requirements
- [ ] Check for existing install script

## Expected Outcome

After audit, determine:
1. **Ready for immediate packaging**: Has releases with binaries
2. **Needs GoReleaser setup**: Has releases but no binaries, or no releases
3. **Needs development work**: Not ready for distribution yet

## Platform Considerations

ntm requires tmux as a runtime dependency:
- macOS: tmux available via Homebrew
- Linux: tmux available via package managers
- Windows: tmux only works in WSL, so Windows native binary may have limited utility

Consider whether Windows Scoop package makes sense given tmux dependency.

## Next Steps (based on audit result)

- If ready: Create Homebrew formula + Scoop manifest tasks
- If needs GoReleaser: Create GoReleaser setup task first
- If not ready: Create development task or mark as deferred","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-13T22:23:34.973471359-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T23:09:50.189572298-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-13T23:09:50.189572298-05:00","close_reason":"AUDIT COMPLETE: ntm is fully ready for package distribution.

FINDINGS:
✓ GoReleaser: Comprehensive config at .goreleaser.yaml with multi-platform builds (Linux/Darwin/Windows/FreeBSD), universal binaries, Homebrew casks, Scoop manifests, nfpm packages, SBOM, and cosign signing
✓ Releases: v1.5.0 is latest (Jan 6, 2026) with all artifacts
✓ Secrets: HOMEBREW_TAP_GITHUB_TOKEN and SCOOP_GITHUB_TOKEN set
✓ Homebrew: Cask at Casks/ntm.rb, auto-generated by GoReleaser
✓ Scoop: Manifest at ntm.json with Windows x64 binary
✓ CLI: Has --version, --help, shell completions
✓ Dependencies: tmux (documented)
✓ README: Full installation section with multiple methods

No action needed - all package distribution infrastructure is complete."}
 {"id":"bd-taoo","title":"Write state management and resume tests","description":"Implements detailed tests for state management, resume, and artifact capture.

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_state_management.sh

## Purpose
Verify state persistence works correctly for resume/restart functionality. Critical for handling interruptions gracefully.

## State File Tests

### State File Creation
- Test state file created at correct location
- Test state file contains all required fields
- Test state file is valid JSON

### State File Updates
- Test state updates as repos complete
- Test failed repos recorded with error reason
- Test timing information captured

### State File Schema
Required fields:
- started_at: ISO timestamp
- repos: array of repo states
- completed_count: number
- failed_count: number
- current_phase: string
- worker_pids: array (parallel mode)

## Resume Tests

### Basic Resume
- Test --resume detects existing state
- Test completed repos are skipped
- Test failed repos are retried
- Test progress continues from last position

### Resume After Interruption
- Simulate Ctrl+C during processing
- Verify state file is complete
- Test resume picks up correctly

### Resume Validation
- Test resume rejects corrupted state
- Test resume warns on stale state (> 24h)
- Test --restart clears state and starts fresh

## Artifact Capture Tests

### Git State Capture
- Test git_status_before captured
- Test git_status_after captured
- Test diff between states computed

### Agent Output Capture
- Test pane_tail captured
- Test commit_plan JSON saved
- Test release_plan JSON saved (if present)

### Artifact Storage
- Test artifacts stored per-repo
- Test artifact directory structure correct
- Test large outputs truncated appropriately

## Logging Requirements
- State file writes logged
- Resume decisions logged
- Artifact capture operations logged
- Any skipped repos explained

## Related Beads
- Tests: bd-hkmt (state file management)
- Tests: bd-7x6h (artifact capture)
- Parent epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] State file created with correct schema
- [ ] State updates atomically (no partial writes)
- [ ] Resume skips completed repos
- [ ] Resume retries failed repos
- [ ] Interrupted runs leave valid state
- [ ] Artifacts captured for each processed repo
- [ ] Large artifacts handled gracefully
- [ ] All state operations have detailed logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:26:55.663565669-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T20:37:13.939297238-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-06T20:37:13.939297238-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented 51 unit tests in scripts/test_state_management.sh covering state initialization, persistence, resume workflow, repo tracking, and artifact capture verification. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-taoo","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.528936906-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-taoo","depends_on_id":"bd-7x6h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.550874918-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-taoo","depends_on_id":"bd-2ze9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:38:02.195432039-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-tfms","title":"Implement agent-sweep command for automated dirty repo processing","description":"## Summary
The `agent-sweep` command is documented in SKILL.md but not yet implemented in ru. This feature would orchestrate AI coding agents to automatically process repositories with uncommitted changes.

## Requirements from SKILL.md

### Basic Usage
```bash
ru agent-sweep              # Process all repos with uncommitted changes
ru agent-sweep --dry-run    # Preview what would be processed
ru agent-sweep -j4          # Process 4 repos in parallel
ru agent-sweep --repos="myproject*"  # Filter to specific repos
ru agent-sweep --with-release  # Include release step after commit
ru agent-sweep --resume     # Resume interrupted sweep
ru agent-sweep --restart    # Start fresh
```

### Three-Phase Agent Workflow
1. **Phase 1: Planning** - Claude Code analyzes uncommitted changes, determines staging, generates commit message
2. **Phase 2: Commit** - Validates plan, stages files, creates commit, runs quality gates, optionally pushes
3. **Phase 3: Release** (optional) - Analyzes history, determines version bump, creates tag/release

### Execution Modes
- `--execution-mode=agent` - Full AI-driven workflow (default)
- `--execution-mode=plan` - Phase 1 only: generate plan, stop
- `--execution-mode=apply` - Phase 2+3: execute existing plan

### Preflight Checks Required
- Is git repository
- Git email configured
- Not a shallow clone
- No rebase/merge in progress
- Not detached HEAD
- Has upstream branch
- Not diverged from upstream

### Security Guardrails
- File denylist (secrets, build artifacts, logs, IDE files)
- Secret scanning (none/warn/block modes)

### Integration Points
- Use ntm robot mode API for session orchestration
- Work-stealing queue for parallel processing
- NDJSON results logging
- Quality gates (auto-detect project type for tests/lint)

## Implementation Notes
- Core prompt for agents should instruct them to read AGENTS.md and README.md
- Logical commit groupings with detailed messages
- Never commit ephemeral files
- Push after successful commits","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-14T01:16:51.741549779-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-14T01:16:51.741549779-05:00"}
 {"id":"bd-tkaa","title":"Review orchestration: fix session start/monitor bugs found during code review","description":"Investigate ru review session orchestration (start/monitor/driver) for argument mismatch, missing state, and ntm integration bugs; fix root causes and add tests if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-08T22:16:51.629445493-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-08T22:35:03.377003845-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-08T22:35:03.377003845-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed review session monitoring/state mapping and driver detection; ran shellcheck and review state unit tests"}

 {"id":"bd-ttm6","title":"Inventory mock/fake usage in tests","description":"# Steps\n- Scan scripts/test_* for PATH mocks (e.g., test_bin).\n- Catalog functions that stub ru functions (source_ru_function + overrides).\n- Note where network/GitHub interactions are mocked.\n\n# Output\n- Table: file -> mocked dependency -> scenario.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:32:55.459959018-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T02:24:46.282059191-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-07T02:24:46.282059191-05:00","close_reason":"Parent audit task bd-m6gs closed - coverage matrix exists via 66 test files, mocks inventory shows only log stubs.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ttm6","depends_on_id":"bd-m6gs","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-07T01:32:55.463695085-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
